text: My dad worked in a bar in the early 80s that pro wrestlers would frequent.  He met Randy Savage (maybe before he had joined WWF, not sure) and described him as very polite and soft-spoken, and definitely an introvert.  I think about that often when considering just how well he was able to perform as Randy Savage.  In a way, it's inspirational to any introverted people who might feel like they struggle with things in life that involve public speaking or acting.  And of course, any introvert is probably a nerd about something and Randy Savage was known to have done voice acting for characters that parodied himself, so I don't think it's a stretch to say that the Macho Man himself would've gotten a chuckle out of this silly YTP.
author: @t3rcx

text:
Ironically, having self-esteem issues, I find public speaking and especially acting to be WAAAAYAAAAYYY easier than just having a normal conversation with one or more people. It's silly but, if I'm acting, I'm technically someone else, so it's all fine because I'm not my actual loser self anymore. 

It doesn't help overcoming the real issue(s) AT ALL, but it's liberating, if anything x)
author: @VonFirflirch

text: @VonFirflirch  literally the only person I’ve found that’s the same way as me. I have super bad social anxiety and no true sense of self so I’m an awkward uncomfortable mess in 1 on 1 conversations, but I can get out on stage and read lines or do a speech in front of 500+ people no problem😭
author: @livybug1101

text:
@livybug1101  
It's incredibly frustrating. You know you're more than capable of conversing, but in some situations, you just feel... utterly disarmed and I hate it.
I don't know how it is for you, but sometimes, I liken strangers/acquaintances to minefields x) "They (probably) don't give a shit about my interests, so what can I even say? ...It's my turn to speak?! Aah! "...Uh, right, yeah, hm-hm.""

Good luck, in any case! I've noticed life isn't quite "designed" with "idiots" like us in mind x)
author: @VonFirflirch

text:
@livybug1101  I'm the same way.  Get up in front of a bunch of people and do an impromptu speech, whatever.  I'm a musician, so going on stage and playing in front of a crowd is fine assuming we've rehearsed and know the setlist.

Go talk to that person over there?  Instant anxiety.  I have no idea why we're wired like this.
author: @doctorbarber1

text: I didn't realize that he was THAT wrestler (whose voice everyone memed) back when he had a website about antique war memorabilia, with useful sections on how to spot fakes, especially fake aging techniques. Yep, he was a genuine nerd alright. He made real contributions to public knowledge on this subject, asking nothing in return. As far as I can remember, there were not even any ads on the site, and his name was in there, but barely. It definitely wasn't for self promotion, as he didn't need it. Just pure enthusiasm for the subject.
author: @noisepuppet

text: ​ @noisepuppet Is there a source for this? Trying to track it down, cus that's genuinely cool lol
author: @yannicreicher6811

text: ​ @yannicreicher6811  it was called Savage Station, if that helps. If nobody has kept it going, it might be on the Wayback Machine. I remember he had articles on fake-aging Confederate Bowie knives with gasoline, stuff like that.
author: @noisepuppet

text: That reminds me of a story my dad told me. He once was a backstage hand at a comedy club and George Carlin did a performance there and after his performance ran into my Dad and he asked him for the playback of his performance. Couple months later my dad receives George Carlin's entire discography signed by George along with a handwritten note thanking him for his help.
